title: Business Breakfast | Stock Market News | June 29, 2026 | TV5 Money Live

recommendations.note: Speculative longs building on lower crude / ATF hopes
recommendations.detail: Kutumba Rao noted speculative longs are mostly building in IndiGo, on the hope that turbine/ATF fuel costs fall as crude prices have come down recently. He flagged it as a positioning observation rather than a firm recommendation.

He grouped Hero Motor with Bajaj Auto as names in focus this week because good monthly auto-sales numbers are due. He wants to see how consumer demand and auto sales hold up despite higher oil prices.
Flagged alongside Hero MotoCorp ahead of this week's automobile sales numbers, which he expects to impact the market. Watching whether sales stay resilient despite higher oil prices.

recommendations.note: Fresh shorts building but possibly a temporary bottom
recommendations.detail: He observed fresh shorts being built in metal stocks including National Aluminium as global rates corrected recently. However he said these look oversold in the extreme short term, so a temporary bottom may be near.

recommendations.note: Fresh shorts building but possibly a temporary bottom
recommendations.detail: Named with National Aluminium as a metal counter where fresh shorts are building on softer global rates. He flagged them as oversold in the very short term, hinting at a possible temporary bottom.

Kutumba Rao said conviction delivery-based buying is visible in HDFC Life, along with Nykaa, Alkem and ICICI Lombard General Insurance. He cited it as a positive institutional-interest signal in his market-internals readout.
Listed among the counters showing conviction delivery-based buying. Flagged as a positive accumulation signal rather than a specific buy call.
Named among stocks where he sees conviction delivery-based buying (with HDFC Life, Nykaa and ICICI Lombard General Insurance). Cited as a positive institutional-interest observation.
Flagged as showing conviction delivery-based buying alongside HDFC Life, Nykaa and Alkem. Presented as a positive market-internals signal, not a firm recommendation.
